action.reasoning: Observation: Iteration 5 finished 40-epoch training with balanced sampling, mixup, and label smoothing (~9.5 min). Validation accuracy is ~67% but training accuracy fluctuated wildly (expected with balanced sampling + mixup). I need the balanced_accuracy of this new model to compare against the current best 0.7275. Analysis: Post-hoc logit adjustment on the previous 15-epoch standard-CE model gave strong gains. If the new model learned better features, combining it with logit adjustment should push the score higher. I must ensure tune_tau.py handles whatever checkpoint format train.py saved (raw state_dict or dict), then tune tau and submit eval. Decision: Update tune_tau.py to robustly load both checkpoint formats and expand tau search to [0.5, 2.5]. Run tuning, smoke-test predict.py, and submit eval with best_adj.pt.
action.command: ls -lh checkpoints/ && python -c "import torch; ckpt=torch.load('checkpoints/best.pt', map_location='cpu'); print('best.pt format:', type(ckpt), list(ckpt.keys())[:5] if isinstance(ckpt, dict) else 'state_dict')" && python tune_tau.py && mkdir -p /tmp/fake_test && cp /data/val.pt /tmp/fake_test/test.pt && python predict.py --data_path /tmp/fake_test --checkpoint checkpoints/best_adj.pt --output /tmp/out.json && python -c "import json; d=json.load(open('/tmp/out.json')); print('Smoke test OK, preds:', len(d['predictions']), d['predictions'][:5])"
